authorization.scope: narrow dead-writer staging-cleanup correction required before automatic cleanup

discovered_gaps.selection: The durability brief authorizes automatic dead-writer staging cleanup, but the frozen contract names only staging/<transaction-id> and does not define an eligible transaction grammar or contents.
discovered_gaps.ownership: The contract does not say which lease proves that no compliant writer still owns a stage, whether a read-only opener may mutate it, or whether PID and age are ownership signals.
discovered_gaps.recovery: Cleanup remove, directory-sync, cancellation, indeterminacy, ordering, and fresh-retry behavior are absent from the transition inventory.
discovered_gaps.risk: A broad recursive or PID-based scavenger could delete a live or foreign tree, follow hostile entries, mutate during read-only open, or claim success without durable removal.

correction.trigger: On existing-store open, cleanup may run only after that session acquired the writer lease, successfully completed bounded read-side store/recovery validation, and reconfirmed the writer against the same held root immediately before removal. It finishes before writable use is exposed or another store mutation runs. A read-only request or writer-contended read-only fallback performs no cleanup. Shared maintenance remains held; staging cleanup requires no exclusive-maintenance upgrade because staging is never read authority.
correction.transaction_id: An eligible direct child of staging is exactly tx-<pid>-<nonce>-<sequence>-<attempt>: pid is canonical minimal decimal in 1..4294967295, nonce is exactly 32 lowercase hexadecimal bytes, sequence is exactly 16 lowercase hexadecimal bytes, and attempt is exactly two lowercase hexadecimal bytes in 00..7f. PID syntax is only a name check; process liveness, age, hostname, and lock-file text are never consulted.
correction.contents: The staging directory, each eligible transaction directory, and each payload share the held store root's filesystem and are reached descriptor-relatively without following links. A transaction is either empty or contains exactly one child named payload. Payload is a single-link regular file no larger than generation_bytes_max. Cleanup never hashes, decodes, or infers the staged payload's intended record kind.
correction.preflight: Before the first removal, completely and boundedly enumerate every direct staging child, reject any name, type, link, device, fan-out, size, descriptor, or capacity violation, and record transaction and optional payload identity facts. One invalid entry rejects the entire cleanup without mutation; valid entries are not selectively scavenged around it.
correction.order: Process the preflighted transaction IDs in unsigned bytewise order. Reopen and revalidate each recorded identity before mutation. When payload exists, remove it and sync the transaction directory. Then remove the now-empty transaction directory and sync the top-level staging directory. If staging exists but was already empty, sync it once without creating or removing anything. Keep the staging directory itself. A missing staging directory succeeds without creation. A final bounded re-inventory must prove every existing staging directory empty.
correction.transitions: Add staging_cleanup_payload_remove, staging_cleanup_directory_sync, and staging_cleanup_transaction_remove. The same staging_cleanup_directory_sync transition is observed once for a transaction directory after payload removal and once for the top-level staging directory after transaction removal. Every repeated occurrence uses the existing before/after and zero-based occurrence injection rule.
correction.cancellation: Cancellation is observed only during the complete preflight, where it returns Cancelled with no mutation. After preflight succeeds, defer cancellation through the entire ordered remove/sync/re-inventory sweep so success never exposes an unsynchronized cleanup prefix.
correction.indeterminate: Any required directory-sync failure, or any other failure after the first successful payload or transaction removal, returns CommitIndeterminate, suspends writes, performs no rollback, and requires close/reopen plus fresh retry. A failure before the first removal changes no namespace bytes.
correction.retry: Fresh retry re-preflights only the currently visible staging namespace. An empty transaction left after payload removal and an absent transaction left after directory removal are valid retry states. An existing empty staging directory receives its required directory sync even on a zero-removal retry; an absent staging directory succeeds without being created. Cleanup is idempotent and no cleanup marker or ownership record is introduced.
correction.scope: Cleanup touches only canonical direct children of the store's internal staging directory. It never removes sibling .mirante4d-project-stage-* packages, published immutable orphans, refs, locks, trash, generations, objects, or the staging directory itself.
correction.claim_boundary: Focused callback and process-crash evidence may prove bounded logic and fresh-process retry. Hostile external replacement between final identity check and unlink, power loss, filesystem qualification, and durability remain separate later evidence.

superseded_fields: PROJECT_STORE_DURABILITY_BRIEF.md unqualified automatic dead-writer staging cleanup sentence
wp10b-project-store-provisional-autosave-correction.json unchanged statement that dead-writer staging cleanup remains later work
unchanged: the sixteen public root exports and every command, completion, fault, receipt, and limit
all authoritative persisted bytes and paths; no marker, ownership file, or new namespace is introduced
dependencies, fixtures, product reachability, and B4 deletion gate
public actor construction, application timers, sibling-package recovery, immutable-orphan compaction, filesystem qualification, and power-loss durability remain later work
evidence: exact correction binding and normalized-contract checks
unchanged public root, dependencies, fixture bytes, authoritative wire, limits, and product reachability
future focused valid, invalid, cancellation, transition, indeterminacy, fresh-process, and idempotent-retry tests
stop_condition: Any public API, export, fault, receipt, or limit change; authoritative byte or path change; marker or ownership record; PID/age/liveness deletion policy; sibling-package, immutable-orphan, lock, or trash cleanup; exclusive-maintenance requirement; dependency, fixture, product wiring, hostile-writer support, filesystem qualification, or durability claim must stop for separate review.
rollback: revert this correction before staging-cleanup implementation; writer-private crash residue remains bounded but is not automatically removed
